expect: GAP-3 Phase 3 (creation-time enforcement): open_lane.py checks for peer swarm lane conflicts at creation time. Expect: structural enforcement detects 100% of frontier collisions between swarms.

actual.inter_swarm: bulletin.py _scan_lane_conflicts() called at creation time — detects peer swarm frontier collisions via bulletin board. Blocks by default (--force to override). Auto-announces lane to bulletin board after creation.
actual.intra_swarm: SWARM-LANES.md scanned for ACTIVE/CLAIMED lanes on same frontier — detects concurrent session conflicts within same swarm. Warning only (not blocking — concurrent work is normal).

test_results.conflict_detection: CONFIRMED — test bulletin created, detected as conflict, cleaned up
test_results.auto_announce: CONFIRMED — write_lane_announce() fires on lane creation
test_results.intra_swarm_scan: CONFIRMED — regex matches frontier= field in Etc column

actual.enforcement_pattern: L-601: voluntary lane-check had 0% adoption. Creation-time enforcement is near-100% (same pattern as --self-apply, --mode, --expect).

diff: Expected 100% detection: CONFIRMED for both inter-swarm and intra-swarm layers. Unexpected addition: intra-swarm detection is more immediately useful than inter-swarm (no peer swarms exist yet, but concurrent sessions are common).
